rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Rescheduling Of Hearing On Defts Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Admission From Caldera Pharamceuticals, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 9, 2014, Line 1: CROSS COMPLAINANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S and LOS ALAMOS NATIONAL SECURITY, L.L.C.'S Notice Of Rescheduling Of Hearing On Defendants Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Requests For Admission From Caldera Pharamceuticals, Inc. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. On October 24, 2013, the parties stipulated to the motion being heard by Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties have advised of tentative settlement. Matters will be continued to March 10, 2014 before this judge pro tem. Parties to file and send to rkane1089@aol.com a joint status conference statement 30 days prior to hearing to advise as to status and if rulings on the motions are necessary. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. 44 NORMANDIE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 9, 2014, Line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT CONSTRUCTION TESTING SERVICES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 5TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Construction Testing Services, Inc's ("CTS") requests for judicial notice granted. The Court did not consider the Declaration of Devin C. Courteau filed in reply to the opposition. For sixth cause of action, the facts pled in the Fifth Amended Cross-Complaint (FAXC) do not establish that CTS owed a duty to Paul Ryan Associates ("PRA"). (See Ratcliff Architects v. Vanir Constr. Mgmt. Inc. (2001) 88 Cal.App.4th 595.) For the seventh cause of action, the facts pled in the FAXC do not state a cause of action because there was no contract between CTS and PRA. "[T]he well settled rule in California is that where the primary objective of a transaction is to obtain services, the doctrines of implied warranty and strict liability do not apply." (Allied Properties v. John A. Blume & Associates (1972) 25 Cal.App.3d 848, 855.) For the eighth cause of action, the FAXC does not state a cause of action because it does not plead that CTS owed a duty to communicate accurate information to PRA. The FAXC does not plead physical harm to a person or property or that there was a business transaction between CTS and PRA. (See Friedman v. Merck & Co. ((2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 454, 483-84.) Additionally, the FAXC does not plead with the requisite specificity what misrepresentations were made. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend in light of the fact that PRA has already had one opportunity to amend these causes of action and has not demonstrated that these defects can be remedied by amendment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR PEREZ VS. DIRECTOR OF THE DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hrg On Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 9, 2014, Line 20, PETITIONER VICTOR PEREZ' hearing On Writ Of Mandate. Writ of mandate denied. In reviewing the record, the trial court exercises its independent judgment, while affording a strong presumption of correctness concerning the administrative findings. (See Lake v. Reed (1997) 16 Cal.4th 448, 456; Fukuda v. City of Angels (1999) 20 Cal.4th 805, 816-817.) The weight of the evidence supports the hearing officer's findings that the peace officer had reasonable cause to stop Petitioner's vehicle and probable cause to make a lawful arrest. Further, the weight of the evidence supports the hearing officer's finding that Petitioner's blood alcohol level was at or above .08% while Petitioner was driving his motor vehicle. The weight of the evidence also supports the finding that Mr. Zehnder's testimony is not credible, and does not rebut the presumption of Vehicle Code § 23152. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIA JUAREZ VS. ANTHONY E NOVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Ipnterrogtories And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 9, 2014, Line 2: PLAINTIFF ELVIA JUAREZ'S Motion To Compel Answers To Ipnterrogtories And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Sanctions of $560. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: UNION BANK, N.A. VS. FRANK MYERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To Form Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 9, 2014, Line 4: PLAINTIFF P & T K PROPERTIES, LLC'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. No opposition filed. Sanctions of $460. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TELEB, AELLA A MINOR BY AN THROUGH HER GAL LILIAN et al VS. WINNIE DUNBAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 9, 2014, Line 12, PLAINTIFF ARNEL TELEB's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Continued to January 30, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 9, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: FATIMA GUILLEN VS. UNIVISION TELEVISION GROUP, INC. A CORPORATION et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 9, 2014, Line 13, PLAINTIFF FATIMA GUILLEN's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. The motion to amend is granted. There is currently no trial date and the liberal policy in favor of amendment outweighs any prejudice to defendant in allowing amendment. rulings.Case Title: PO ON DONG LU BENEVOLENT ASSOCIATION VS. YUNG TIM CHEUK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Def Yung Tim Cheuk'S Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 9, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANTs YUNG CHEUK & Yung Tim Cheuk'S motion For Leave To File cross-Complaint. Granted. Defendant's proposed Cross-Complaint is compulsory. Given the liberal policy to permit the filing of cross-complaint, the motion is granted. (CCP § 426.50.) The trial date of March 10, 2014 is vacated, and the new trial date is set for September 8, 2014. The discovery cutoff shall be determined in accordance with the new trial date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DAVID CHEUNG et al VS. SANDIE W.N.Y. CHEUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 9, 2014, Line 17, DEFENDANTs SANDIE CHEUNG, HENRY TSAI, LUNG SHING CO., LLC & LUNG SHING INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled in part and sustained in part, with leave to amend. Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice is granted as to Exhibit A to the extent that the Court takes judicial notice that a dissolution action is pending in San Mateo County Superior Court between Plaintiff Cheung and Defendant Cheung; granted as to Exh. B. (complaint in this action) and denied as to Exhibit C (purported contemporaneous business records that are subject to dispute and cannot be the basis for ruling on the merits of the demurrer). As to the demurrer to the entire complaint, defendants have not shown that this lawsuit concerns the same matters involved in the ongoing dissolution action in San Mateo County. As to the first, third, and fourth causes of action, Defendants have not established that the face of the complaint establishes that these causes of action are barred by the applicable statute of limitations; the Court does not consider the evidence attached as Exhibit C to the request for judicial notice. As to the second cause of action, constructive trust is a remedy. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to replead this cause of action as a remedy. As to the fifth cause of action, civil conspiracy is not a separate cause of action. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to replead this cause of action as part of the general charging allegations of the complaint. To the extent not otherwise stated, the balance of the demurrer is overruled. This Order is without prejudice to Defendants seeking a stay or to consolidate this matter with the family law case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
